# Graphwise Analysis Reference

Use this file when the task involves reading `<output_prefix>_report.md` generated by XSlim with `analysis_enable: true`.

## Report Columns

| Column | Meaning | Healthy Reference |
|---|---|:---:|
| `Op` | Operator name and type | — |
| `Var` | Tensor name | — |
| `SNR` | Quantization noise / signal energy ratio | `< 0.1` |
| `MSE` | Mean squared error | lower is better |
| `Cosine` | Cosine similarity between float and quantized values | `> 0.99` |
| `Q.MinMax` | Quantized tensor range | — |
| `F.MinMax` | Float tensor range | — |
| `F.Hist` | Float histogram distribution | — |

## Threshold Guidance

### SNR

- `SNR < 0.01`: negligible error
- `0.01 <= SNR < 0.1`: usually acceptable
- `SNR >= 0.1`: priority hotspot

### Cosine

- `Cosine > 0.999`: excellent
- `0.99 <= Cosine <= 0.999`: slight deviation
- `Cosine < 0.99`: likely needs attention

## Reading Patterns

### Local hotspot

Symptoms:

- one or two rows with red SNR / Cosine
- most other rows look healthy

Likely action:

- local `custom_setting`
- targeted calibration change
- operator exclusion for the affected subgraph or operator type

### Global issue

Symptoms:

- many rows show moderate error
- no single operator dominates

Likely action:

- raise `finetune_level`
- re-check preprocessing
- improve calibration dataset quality
- consider a higher global `precision_level`

### Range clipping

Symptoms:

- `F.MinMax` is much wider than `Q.MinMax`

Likely action:

- change `calibration_type`
- adjust `max_percentile`
- apply local calibration override in `custom_setting`

### Sparse histogram

Symptoms:

- many zero bins in `F.Hist`

Likely action:

- increase `calibration_step`
- improve calibration dataset diversity

## Fast Reading Checklist

1. sort attention by highest SNR / lowest Cosine rows
2. check whether bad rows cluster by operator type or subgraph
3. compare float and quantized ranges
4. inspect histogram shape for outliers or sparse data
5. decide whether the issue is global or local before editing config
